title: Smoky Middle Eastern Lamb Skewers
description: Sear tender lamb cubes infused with smoky cumin and aromatic star anise, finished on the grill for a fragrant, halal-friendly dish that energizes any meal.
instructions: In a bowl, combine olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, cinnamon, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to form a thick rub.
Add lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, preferably overnight, to allow flavors to meld.
Preheat a charcoal or gas grill to medium-high heat (about 400°F / 200°C). While the grill heats, ignite the star anise over a small flame to release its aroma, then place it on the grill grates.
Thread 3–4 lamb cubes onto each soaked skewer, packing tightly but leaving a little space for juices to escape.
Brush the skewers with a little olive oil and sprinkle a pinch of the spice rub over the tops.

Place the skewers on the grill, turning every 3–4 minutes, until the lamb is nicely seared on the outside and reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare, about 10–12 minutes total.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for 5 minutes; this allows the juices to redistribute and keeps the meat moist.
While resting, lightly drizzle the grilled star anise over the skewers to impart a subtle smoky fragrance.
Serve the lamb skewers hot, accompanied by a side of warm flatbread, a cucumber‑yogurt salad, and a drizzle of tahini sauce.
For leftover portions, store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days and reheat gently on the grill or in a skillet.
